title: Anime movie order for a new fan
display_title: Anime movie order for a new fan
wikitext:
[]: Anime/movie order for a new fan?             Quote Post by stripywitch » Wed Feb 02, 2011 3:19 pm I've never liked humongous mecha animes, well, I've never really watched any anime since I was about 16-17. I happened to read an entry about Rebuild on a TV Tropes page and I just decided to watch it to see what all the fuss is about. So yeah, I'm a new fan and I've just watched 1.0, 2.0 (or 1.11... or... whatever ;o) and episodes 7-24 of the anime. My question is, should I bother watching 1-6? Should I bother watching 25 and 26? I ask this because I read that the older NGE movies are remakes of the last 2 episodes, and that 1.0 is a remake of 1-6. Also, I heard that Death, Rebirth and End were all remade into Revival of Evangelion, so should I just watch that one, or...? Which order should I watch these in? Also: I was pleasantly surprised to see the origin of all the giant cross explosions from Disgaea. xP

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Wed Feb 02, 2011 3:30 pm Yes, watching eps 1-6 is worth it. To great extent Rebuild 1.0 just copied those episodes (the Ramiel fight however is all new and absolutely terrific) but there's enough differences and additions to make it worth it. Most notably episode 4 was skipped nearly entirely in Rebuild, replaced by montage. I hope you remembered to watch Director's Cut of eps 21-24! Yes, watching "End of TV" (EoTV) aka eps 25-26 is worth it and a must for eva fan. You haven't got full Evangelion experience otherwise Death & Rebirth you can watch or skip, it doesn't really matter as long as you watched Director's Cut versions of 21-24. D&R was basically just clip show summary of tv series + first half hour of End of Evangelion. The new scenes/elements were incorporated back to the tv series in form of Director's Cut episodes :) But definetly watch End of Evangelion! Only thing skippable here is D&R. All else is must see :)

[]:             Quote Post by stripywitch » Wed Feb 02, 2011 3:38 pm I watched the TV Anime online (I won't post the link incase it's detected as spam or something), the latest episodes have been a few minutes longer so I'm guessing they're the director's cuts, how much footage was added cause it doesn't seem like a whole movie's worth.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Feb 02, 2011 3:46 pm My recommendation: 1-26 (TV Series) After the End (Addition Audio Drama) 21'-24' (Director's Cuts) The End of Evangelion Alternate Live Action Sequence ReDeath One Hour Photo 1.11, 2.22 (New Movies) Petit Eva: Evangelion@School Six months from now when you feel like watching The End of Evangelion but don't feel like watching the entire series: Death

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Feb 02, 2011 3:52 pm The easy way to detect DC versions of 21' -- starts with a flashback to the UN Antarctic base 22' -- starts with a flashback to Asuka and Kaji the night before episode 8 23' -- hard to describe without a spoiler, but there's some unusual spectacle before the Angel explodes (as they do) 24' -- Gendo soliloquises in front of EVA-01

[]:             Quote Post by stripywitch » Wed Feb 02, 2011 4:10 pm SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Wed Feb 02, 2011 4:11 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: My recommendation: 1-26 (TV Series) After the End (Addition Audio Drama) 21'-24' (Director's Cuts) The End of Evangelion

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Wed Feb 02, 2011 4:28 pm I wouldn't even bother watching non-DC versions of episodes 21-24 because the director's cut is frankly superior. No reason to watch something you'll see again only with worse animation. And EoE is required viewing.

[]:             Quote Post by stripywitch » Wed Feb 02, 2011 4:30 pm I don't think there was. It must have been a regular episode then. I kind of feel confused and spoiled by the whole Adam/Lilith thing. I already knew that the angel in the basement of NERV was Lilith (from 1.0), but it looked a little different from her so I thought, hey, maybe it is Adam. Does the fact that Ka(w)oru wanted to die after finding out "Adam" was Lilith mean that all of the angels are mistaken and therefore have no reason to attack? I'm confused.

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Wed Feb 02, 2011 4:32 pm Make sure you see the Director's Cuts of 21-24. Watch episodes 25 and 26, but watch End of Evangelion, too. They go together.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Feb 02, 2011 4:37 pm Xard wrote: I really don't see a reason to watch On Air version beforehand.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Wed Feb 02, 2011 4:41 pm stripywitch wrote: I don't think there was. It must have been a regular episode then.

[]:             Quote Post by liquidus118 » Wed Feb 02, 2011 5:06 pm Xard wrote: Really simple: did you see this in ep 23 ? it was sort of brownish

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Wed Feb 02, 2011 5:09 pm We really need a definitive thread for this viewing order/spin-off business..

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Wed Feb 02, 2011 6:54 pm /a/ made this guide when 2.22 came out on BD/DVD and eva threads were everywhere for at least week or two and it quickly spread to quite wide use. It's pretty good though of course us hardcore fans will get soreassed about thing or two in the description part and I'd personally say watching EoTV before EoE makes much more sense than other way around. http://img145.imageshack.us/i/watchingngeaguide.png

[]:             Quote Post by stripywitch » Wed Feb 02, 2011 6:54 pm I didn't see it, and that's a good idea InstrumentalityOne. Okay I finished the last episodes. I'm not gonna lie... I have no idea what I just witnessed.

[]:             Quote Post by BiQ » Wed Feb 02, 2011 7:50 pm stripywitch wrote: Okay I finished the last episodes. I'm not gonna lie... I have no idea what I just witnessed.

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Wed Feb 02, 2011 8:21 pm stripywitch wrote: Okay I finished the last episodes. I'm not gonna lie... I have no idea what I just witnessed.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Feb 02, 2011 8:40 pm Trajan wrote: There are two types of Eva fans...those who are confused by 25 and 26 and those who claim not to be.

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Wed Feb 02, 2011 9:55 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: I think that 25 and 26 are more straightforward than The End of Evangelion by a longshot.
